intro: PHP's web-centric execution model and dynamic typing share similarities with Python, but the idioms diverge significantly. PHP's `$` variable prefix and array functions (`array_map`, `array_filter`) translate to Python's plain variables and built-in `list` methods. PHP's mixed-type arrays become Python lists or dicts, `echo` statements become `print`, and PHP's `require`/`include` becomes Python's `import` system.

diffs.to: No prefix. Plain names: name, count, user.

diffs.from: Weakly typed; automatic coercion between strings and numbers.
diffs.to: Dynamically typed; explicit conversion required (int("5") not "5" + 1).

diffs.from: Single array type covers indexed lists, associative maps, and mixes.
diffs.to: list for sequences, dict for key-value maps, set for unique values.

diffs.from: Runs natively inside web servers (Apache/nginx). echo outputs HTML.
diffs.to: General-purpose. Use Flask/Django/FastAPI for web. print() to stdout.

diffs.from: Older code uses error codes/warnings; modern PHP uses try/catch.
diffs.to: Pure exception-based. Every error is an exception.

examples.title: Array functions → list operations
examples.description: PHP's array_map/array_filter become Python list comprehensions or built-in functions.
examples.toCode:
numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]

squared = [n ** 2 for n in numbers if n % 2 == 0]

print(', '.join(str(n) for n in squared))  # 4, 16, 36, 64

examples.title: PHP class → Python class
examples.description: PHP visibility modifiers and type hints map cleanly to Python. The $ prefix and -> arrow disappear.

tips.title: Strip the $ prefix from every variable
tips.body: PHP's $variable syntax is purely syntactic. Remove the $ from every identifier. $userList becomes user_list; $totalCount becomes total_count.

tips.title: Split PHP arrays into list or dict
tips.body: If PHP array uses sequential integer keys, use Python list. If it uses string keys (associative), use dict. Mixed arrays should be refactored.

tips.title: array_* functions → comprehensions
tips.body: array_map($fn, $arr) → [fn(x) for x in arr]. array_filter($arr, $fn) → [x for x in arr if fn(x)]. array_reduce → functools.reduce.

tips.title: echo / print → print()
tips.body: PHP echo and print statements become Python's print() function. String concatenation with . becomes + or f-strings.
